growth_records.cite: Raulf et al. The structure of the Vibrio natriegens 70S ribosome in complex with the proline-rich antimicrobial peptide Bac5(1–17). Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf324. [PMC12056610]
growth_records.snippet: there has been growing interest in V. natriegens as an alternative system due to its rapid growth rate, with a doubling time of 10 min, thus, exceeding E. coli by a factor of 2–3-fold
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 10 min for V. natriegens is stated in the paper's Introduction but is a literature value cited from refs [24,25], not a new measurement made in this study. mu = ln2/doubling_time = ln2/(10/60 h) = 4.16 h^-1 (calculated by curator from the stated doubling time). Medium (BHI+2.5% NaCl, 37C, aerobic) is the growth condition used elsewhere in this paper's own methods (growth-inhibition assay / mid-log harvest) for V. natriegens ATCC14048, but the 10-min doubling time itself is not tied to a specific medium/composition measurement in this paper.

growth_records.strain: OFS003 (ancestor; TND1964 pMMB-tfoX WT-derived, Δdns::KanR, ΔflgE, non-motile)

up.units: not quantified as specific rate; fermented (glucose exhausted within ~24 h, HPLC)
sole N source for ancestor; dissolved N2 ~622 uM (Henry's law, 1.02 atm, 6.1e-4 M/atm); km_N2 = 0.1 mM

sec.units: accumulated then depleted; not quantified as specific rate

growth_records.cite: Schakel et al. Microbial cross-feeding stabilized by segregation of a dependent mutant from its independent ancestor. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf131. [PMC12279053]
growth_records.snippet: Parameter values: ... mu_max with NH4+, 0.43 h-1; mu_max with N2, 0.25 h-1; km_NH4+, 0.01 mM; km_N2, 0.1 mM. ... Monod model estimates of V. natriegens ancestor and mutant growth rates.
growth_records.notes: V. natriegens ancestor fixes N2; its maximum specific growth rate on N2 is the Monod mu_max_N2 = 0.25 h-1 (experimentally-grounded model parameter, Methods/Fig 1). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.25 = 2.77 h. Substrate concentrations from Methods: dissolved N2 = 622 uM (Henry's law, 1.02 atm x 6.1e-4 M/atm); km_N2 = 0.1 mM. Glucose fermentation confirmed by HPLC (glucose exhausted ~24 h; organic acids/ethanol excreted) but no numeric specific rates reported. MDC base salt composition referenced to ref 23, not restated; stated modifications listed.

growth_records.strain: OFS004 (LOF mutant; ΔnifA, Δdns::SpecR, ΔflgE, non-motile; NH4+-dependent)

up.units: fermented; not quantified as specific rate
sole N source at saturation; km_NH4+ = 0.01 mM; in coculture NH4+ sub-saturating (~2 uM per OD660, producer excretion 6.5e-10 umol/cell)

sec.units: fermentation products; not quantified as specific rate

growth_records.cite: Schakel et al. Microbial cross-feeding stabilized by segregation of a dependent mutant from its independent ancestor. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf131. [PMC12279053]
growth_records.snippet: mu_max with NH4+, 0.43 h-1; ... km_NH4+, 0.01 mM ... At the experimentally-estimated NH4+ excretion level of 6.5 x 10-10 umol cell-1 ... NH4+, 2 uM based on 20 uM R. palustris OD660-1.
growth_records.notes: V. natriegens LOF mutant (ΔnifA) grows on NH4+; maximum specific growth rate at saturating NH4+ is Monod mu_max_NH4+ = 0.43 h-1 (Methods/Fig 1 model parameter). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/0.43 = 1.61 h. km_NH4+ = 0.01 mM. In coculture NH4+ is sub-saturating (producer excretes 6.5e-10 umol/cell; ~2 uM NH4+ per unit OD660), so realized mutant rate is far below 0.43 h-1; the value recorded is the saturating maximum. Same genetic background as ancestor except ΔnifA + resistance-cassette swap (comparable cassette effect on growth, Fig S2).

growth_records.cite: Zhu et al. Shaping of microbial phenotypes by trade-offs. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-48591-9. [PMC11102524]
growth_records.snippet: The maximal growth capacities of bacteria across species vary substantially with the shortest generation time ranging from 10 min (e.g. Vibrio natriegens)
growth_records.notes: Review-cited generic shortest generation time, not a primary measurement; no medium or conditions stated. td=10 min=10/60=0.16667 h; mu=ln2/td=0.6931/0.16667=4.159 /h. No uptake/secretion data reported.

up.method: quantified formate depletion; paper states all formate exhausted within 24 h

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: V. natriegens could grow in the LBv2 medium supplemented with 20 and 40 g·L−1 sodium formate (HCOONa·2H2O) (equivalent to 192 and 385 mM formate, respectively) ... and exhausted all the formate within 24 h
growth_records.notes: 20 g/L HCOONa·2H2O = 192 mM formate (paper). Volumetric uptake computed = 192 mM / 24 h = 8.0 mM/h; this is an average lower bound because formate was 'exhausted within 24 h' (may have finished earlier). No gDW biomass reported for this condition, so per-biomass qS not derivable. Doubling time <10 min mentioned for the species is CITED (refs 21,22), not measured here.

up.method: formate depletion measured; exhausted within 24 h

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: V. natriegens could grow in the LBv2 medium supplemented with 20 and 40 g·L−1 sodium formate ... (equivalent to 192 and 385 mM formate) ... and exhausted all the formate within 24 h, although the growth rates and highest biomass gradually d
growth_records.notes: 40 g/L HCOONa·2H2O = 385 mM formate (paper). Volumetric uptake = 385 mM / 24 h = 16.04 mM/h (average lower bound; 'exhausted within 24 h'). No gDW biomass reported so per-biomass qS not derivable.

up.method: measured 69 mM formate consumed within 24 h

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: At a higher sodium formate concentration (60 g·L−1, equivalent to 577 mM), the strain could hardly grow, but it still consumed 69 mM formate within 24 h
growth_records.notes: 60 g/L HCOONa·2H2O = 577 mM formate. Measured consumption 69 mM in 24 h => volumetric uptake = 69/24 = 2.875 mM/h. Growth strongly inhibited (essentially no growth), so per-biomass rate not meaningful/derivable.

up.method: measured 95 mM formate consumed within 24 h

up.units: g/L (4 g/L supplied, consumption rate not reported)
up.method: present as growth substrate; uptake rate not quantified

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: At a lower formate concentration (192 mM ... 20 g·L−1 HCOONa·2H2O) in this minimal medium, V. natriegens could grow and consume 95 mM formate within 24 h (3.96 mM·h−1)
growth_records.notes: Paper explicitly reports volumetric formate uptake 3.96 mM/h (=95 mM / 24 h). Confirms: 95/24 = 3.958 ≈ 3.96 mM/h. M9 base composition beyond the stated additions (2% NaCl, 4 g/L glucose, 0.25 g/L glycine when needed) not itemized. At 288 mM formate in this M9 medium growth was completely inhibited (no record).

growth_records.strain: S-TCA-2.0 (engineered S-TCA loop + adaptive laboratory evolution)

up.units: mmol/L/h (volumetric; 1.42 g/L/h, both stated in paper)
up.method: measured 78.9 g/L (759 mM) sodium formate consumed within 24 h

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: S-TCA-2.0 ... capable of growing to OD600 ~ 1.6 with the consumption of 78.9 g·L−1 sodium formate (equivalent to 759 mM formate) within 24 h ... reaching a formate consumption rate of 1.42 g·L−1·h−1 (31.6 mM·h−1)
growth_records.notes: Engineered+evolved strain but rates are directly MEASURED. Paper states volumetric formate uptake = 1.42 g/L/h = 31.6 mM/h (=759 mM / 24 h; check 759/24 = 31.6 mM/h ✓). Endpoint biomass OD600 ~1.6 at 24 h; growth rate (mu) not derivable from single endpoint. No gDW conversion given.

up.method: 13C isotopomer analysis; specific assimilation rate derived from specific consumption rates of formate, glucose and glycine

up.units: specific consumption rate measured (value in Supplementary Table 2, not in main text)
specific consumption rate measured (value in Supplementary Table 2, not in main text)

mfa.note: = 12.1% of total consumed formate; remainder dissimilated (oxidized to CO2) or secreted. Derived from 13C-isotopomer + specific rates of formate/glucose/glycine (Supplementary Table 2).

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: Based on the specific consumption rates of formate, glucose, and glycine, the specific formate assimilation rate was calculated to be 43.3 mg·gDCW−1·h−1, accounting for approximate 12.1% of the total consumed formate
growth_records.notes: This is the only per-biomass (gDCW) rate in the paper: specific formate ASSIMILATION (into biomass) = 43.3 mg/gDCW/h = 12.1% of total consumed formate. Implied total specific formate consumption ≈ 43.3/0.121 = 357.9 mg/gDCW/h (≈ 7.78 mmol/gDCW/h using formate MW 46; arithmetic shown, but not stated by authors). Underlying specific consumption rates of formate/glucose/glycine are in Supplementary Table 2 (not in main XML).

growth_records.strain: S-TCA-2.0-IE (S-TCA-2.0 + Streptomyces idgs / B. subtilis sfp indigoidine pathway)

up.method: measured total ~165.3 g/L formate consumed over 72 h fed-batch

sec.method: measured 29.0 g/L indigoidine produced within 72 h

product_yields.indigoidine_g_per_g_formate: ~0.175 g/g (29.0 g/L indigoidine / 165.3 g/L formate consumed; derived, formate co-fed with LB nutrients so not sole carbon source)

growth_records.cite: Tian, Jinzhong et al. Discovery and remodeling of Vibrio natriegens as a microbial platform for efficient formic acid biorefinery. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43631-2. [PMC10682008]
growth_records.snippet: S-TCA-2.0-IE consumed all the formate (approximately 165.3 g·L−1 formate) and produced 29.0 g·L−1 of indigoidine within 72 h ... achieving a specific formate consumption rate of 2.3 g·L−1·h−1
growth_records.notes: Fed-batch: initial 60 g/L + 60 g/L x5 feeds. Paper states volumetric formate consumption 2.3 g/L/h and 29.0 g/L indigoidine in 72 h; peak OD600 ~3.1 at 60 h. Indigoidine avg productivity computed = 29.0 g/L / 72 h = 0.403 g/L/h. Product yield computed = 29.0/165.3 = 0.175 g indigoidine per g formate (NOTE: LBv2 also supplies LB nutrients as carbon/N, so formate is a co-substrate, not sole source — yield is nominal). Without formate feeding the same strain made only 4.7 g/L indigoidine (OD600 ~1.5).

up.method: CITED from Hoffart et al. 2017 (Appl Environ Microbiol / Metab Eng) and Long et al. 2017; aerobic minimal glucose medium; reported as 3.9 g gDW-1 h-1
CITED from Hoffart et al. 2017; used by authors to constrain in silico glucose uptake

sec.method: CITED from Hoffart et al. 2017; reported as 1.4 g gDW-1 h-1 acetate during exponential growth on minimal medium (used to constrain in silico acetate secretion)

yields.Yxs: 0.41 g/g glucose (model-predicted); 0.38 g/g glucose reported experimentally by Hoffart et al. 2017 (cited)

growth_records.cite: Coppens et al. Vibrio natriegens genome-scale modeling reveals insights into halophilic adaptations and resource allocation. Molecular Systems Biology (2023). DOI:10.15252/msb.202110523. [PMC10090949]
growth_records.method: reported (measured; growth rate from OD600 exponential phase, Table 2, 1.66 +/- 0.07 h-1)
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 ... 300 mM 21.9% ribosomes, 1.66 (+/- 0.07) h-1. The glucose uptake rate of V. natriegens during aerobic growth on minimal medium was independently experimentally determined to be 3.9 g gDW-1 h-1 by Hoffart et al (2017) and Long et
growth_records.notes: Growth rate 1.66 h-1 MEASURED in this study (Table 2, proteomic sample at 300 mM NaCl, mod-M9 + 1% glucose, 36.5C, aerobic). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/1.66 = 0.4176 h. Glucose uptake and acetate secretion rates are NOT measured in this paper; they are CITED from Hoffart et al. 2017 (3.9 g gDW-1 h-1 glucose; 1.4 g gDW-1 h-1 acetate) for comparable aerobic minimal-glucose growth. Unit conversions: glucose 3.9 g gDW-1 h-1 / 180.16 g/mol = 0.021647 mol = 21.65 mmol/gDW/h; acetate 1.4 g gDW-1 h-1 / 60.05 g/mol = 0.023314 mol = 23.31 mmol/gDW/h. Note an alternate acetate figure of 0.42 g gDW-1 is also cited from Hoffart. Model iLC858. Genome accession CP009977.1/CP009978.1.

growth_records.cite: Coppens et al. Vibrio natriegens genome-scale modeling reveals insights into halophilic adaptations and resource allocation. Molecular Systems Biology (2023). DOI:10.15252/msb.202110523. [PMC10090949]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 ... 540 mM 22.6 (+/- 0.5)% ribosomes, corresponding growth rate 1.51 (+/- 0.02) h-1.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate 1.51 h-1 MEASURED in this study at 540 mM NaCl (mod-M9 + 1% glucose, 36.5C, aerobic). doubling_time = ln2/1.51 = 0.459 h. No uptake/secretion measured at this salinity.

growth_records.cite: Coppens et al. Vibrio natriegens genome-scale modeling reveals insights into halophilic adaptations and resource allocation. Molecular Systems Biology (2023). DOI:10.15252/msb.202110523. [PMC10090949]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 ... 650 mM 21.6 (+/- 0.1)% ribosomes, corresponding growth rate 1.16 (+/- 0.06) h-1.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate 1.16 h-1 MEASURED in this study at 650 mM NaCl (mod-M9 + 1% glucose, 36.5C, aerobic). doubling_time = ln2/1.16 = 0.5975 h. Growth rate declines with increasing salinity (1.66 -> 1.51 -> 1.16 h-1 at 300/540/650 mM). A 0 mM NaCl condition was also grown (Dataset EV6) but its growth rate is not tabulated in the article text.

growth_records.medium: Minimal glucose medium (anaerobic) - values cited from Hoffart et al. 2017

up.method: CITED from Hoffart et al. 2017; reported as 7.81 g gDW-1 h-1 anaerobic glucose uptake

sec.method: Anaerobic by-product experimentally detected (secreted); cited/confirmed vs Hoffart et al. 2017
Anaerobic by-product secreted; cited vs Hoffart et al. 2017
Anaerobic by-product secreted; cited vs Hoffart et al. 2017

growth_records.cite: Coppens et al. Vibrio natriegens genome-scale modeling reveals insights into halophilic adaptations and resource allocation. Molecular Systems Biology (2023). DOI:10.15252/msb.202110523. [PMC10090949]
growth_records.snippet: Hoffart et al (2017), who quantified a substrate uptake rate of 7.81 g gDW-1 h-1 and a growth rate of 0.92 h-1 during anaerobic growth on minimal medium. Additionally, the model predicted anaerobic by-products formate, acetate, and lactate
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic physiology CITED from Hoffart et al. 2017 (not measured in this paper): glucose uptake 7.81 g gDW-1 h-1, growth rate 0.92 h-1; mixed-acid fermentation products formate/acetate/lactate secreted (no per-product rates given in this article). doubling_time = ln2/0.92 = 0.7534 h. Unit conversion: 7.81 g gDW-1 h-1 / 180.16 g/mol = 0.043350 mol = 43.35 mmol/gDW/h. Included for provenance/completeness; all values attributed to the cited primary source, so confidence set to medium. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH]

growth_records.cite: Failmezger, Jurek et al. Cell-Free Protein Synthesis From Fast-Growing Vibrio natriegens. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.01146. [PMC5992293]
growth_records.method: reported (differential specific growth rate measured, Figure 1A); doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: high specific growth rates up to 3 h-1 were obtained in shake flask cultivations performed at 37C (Figure 1A). The biomass was harvested at maximum growth after only 2 h of cultivation
growth_records.notes: Experimental prokaryote growth: V. natriegens in BHIN, 37C, shake flask (batch), aerobic; measured differential specific growth rate up to 3 h-1 (Figure 1A). Doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.693/3.0=0.231 h (~13.9 min). Medium is complex (brain-heart infusion + NaCl); composition not itemized. The paper additionally CITES a higher rate from another paper: Hoffart et al. 2017 reported differential growth rates up to 4.43 h-1 (td 9.4 min) in complex medium; and Aiyar et al. 2002 estimated ~115,000 ribosomes/cell at td ~15 min. These cited values are NOT this paper's own measurement and are not made into separate records. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates were measured for growing cells (amino-acid consumption data in this paper are from the in-vitro cell-free extract reaction, not from growth).

growth_records.cite: Schulze, Clarissa et al. Investigation of exopolysaccharide formation and its impact on anaerobic succinate production with Vibrio natriegens. Microbial Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.14277. [PMC10832516]
growth_records.snippet: During the batch phase, V. natriegens grew at maximum growth rate of 1.67 +/- 0.01 h-1 and showed a biomass yield (YX/S) of 0.49 +/- 0.01 gX gS-1 and a biomass specific glucose consumption rate (qS) of 3.44 +/- 0.10 gS gX-1 h-1
growth_records.notes: mu=1.67 h-1 (regression of ln CDW), Yxs=0.49 gX/gS, qS=3.44 gS/gX/h all reported (Table 2), n=3. doubling=ln2/1.67=0.415 h. qS in molar: 3.44/0.18016=19.09 mmol glucose/gDW/h. CDW=0.3*OD600. Up to 1.2 g/L acetate accumulated in batch, reutilized in feed phase.

growth_records.cite: Schulze, Clarissa et al. Investigation of exopolysaccharide formation and its impact on anaerobic succinate production with Vibrio natriegens. Microbial Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.14277. [PMC10832516]

up.method: from Qp 7.5 g/L/h and Yps 1.1 mol/mol: 45 g suc/6 h then glucose = succinate_mol/Yps

growth_records.cite: Schulze, Clarissa et al. Investigation of exopolysaccharide formation and its impact on anaerobic succinate production with Vibrio natriegens. Microbial Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.14277. [PMC10832516]
growth_records.snippet: V. natriegens Succ1 reached a final titre of 45 g succinate L-1 within 6 h, showed a product yield YP/S of 1.1 molSuc molGlc-1 and a volumetric productivity QP of 7.5 gSuc L-1 h-1
growth_records.notes: Zero-growth resting-cell process, n=1. Succinate 45 g/L in 6 h -> Qp=7.5 g/L/h (reported). Yps=1.1 mol/mol. Glucose uptake derived: 45 g/118.09 g/mol=0.381 mol/L succinate; glucose=0.381/1.1=0.346 mol/L=62.4 g/L over 6 h=10.4 g/L/h volumetric; /17 gX/L=0.61 g/gDW/h. mu=0 (resting/zero-growth). Reference process from Thoma et al. 2021.

up.method: from Qp 8.5 and Yps 1.4: succinate 51 g/L/6 h; glucose=succinate_mol/1.4

yields.Yps: ~1.4 mol succinate/mol glucose (81% of theoretical max 1.71)

growth_records.cite: Schulze, Clarissa et al. Investigation of exopolysaccharide formation and its impact on anaerobic succinate production with Vibrio natriegens. Microbial Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.14277. [PMC10832516]
growth_records.snippet: V. natriegens Succ1 delta-cpsR and Succ1 delta-cps achieved final succinate concentrations of 51 and 46 g L-1 with a QP of 8.5 g L-1 h-1 and 7.7 g L-1 h-1... both strains exhibited a YP/S of around 1.4 molSuc molGlc-1, which is 27% higher..
growth_records.notes: Zero-growth resting-cell, n=1. Succinate 51 g/L, Qp(avg)=8.5 g/L/h, max Qp=19.4 g/L/h reported. Yps~1.4 mol/mol=81% of theoretical 1.71. Glucose uptake derived: 51/118.09=0.432 mol/L; /1.4=0.309 mol/L=55.6 g/L over 6 h=9.3 g/L/h volumetric. mu=0 (resting).

up.method: from Qp 7.7 and Yps 1.4: succinate 46 g/L/6 h; glucose=succinate_mol/1.4

yields.Yps: ~1.4 mol succinate/mol glucose (81% of theoretical max 1.71)

growth_records.cite: Schulze, Clarissa et al. Investigation of exopolysaccharide formation and its impact on anaerobic succinate production with Vibrio natriegens. Microbial Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.14277. [PMC10832516]
growth_records.snippet: Succ1 delta-cps achieved final succinate concentration of 46 g L-1 with a QP of 7.7 gSuc L-1 h-1; YP/S of around 1.4 molSuc molGlc-1, 81% of theoretical maximum
growth_records.notes: Zero-growth resting-cell, n=1. Succinate 46 g/L, Qp=7.7 g/L/h. Yps~1.4 mol/mol. Glucose uptake derived: 46/118.09=0.390 mol/L; /1.4=0.278 mol/L=50.1 g/L over 6 h=8.3 g/L/h. mu=0.
